hypothesis:
Based on the identified gap that only 61% of newly discovered superhydrides exhibit Tc > 100 K despite containing 3D hydrogen clathrate structures, I propose:

Incorporating transition metals with partially filled d-orbitals into the metal lattice sites of identified clathrate frameworks will enhance electron-phonon coupling at the Fermi level beyond the current hydrogen-dominated contribution, thereby increasing the superconducting transition temperature above 200 K in at least 40% of the modified structures.
